suites.name: Core Functionality
suites.file: initial-page-load.spec.ts, add-single-element.spec.ts, add-multiple-elements.spec.ts, delete-single-element.spec.ts, delete-all-elements.spec.ts, url-stability.spec.ts

suites.name: Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions
suites.file: rapid-click-add.spec.ts, rapid-click-delete.spec.ts, add-large-number.spec.ts, delete-non-sequential.spec.ts, add-after-delete.spec.ts, delete-first-repeatedly.spec.ts, delete-last-repeatedly.spec.ts, alternating-add-delete.spec.ts, delete-all-clean-state.spec.ts

suites.name: Negative and Validation Tests
suites.file: double-click-delete.spec.ts, click-container.spec.ts, no-add-then-delete.spec.ts, no-maximum-limit.spec.ts, browser-back-button.spec.ts, page-refresh-reset.spec.ts, no-console-errors.spec.ts, button-rendering.spec.ts, keyboard-add-element.spec.ts, keyboard-delete-button.spec.ts

use_cases.CORE: Core Functionality — verifies basic add/remove element operations, page load state, and URL stability
use_cases.EDGE: Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions — tests rapid clicking, large numbers of elements, non-sequential deletion, and mixed add/delete patterns
use_cases.NEG: Negative and Validation Tests — verifies double-click behavior, container clicks, state persistence, console errors, rendering, and keyboard accessibility

tests.title: Initial Page Load State
tests.note: Healed: replaced toBeVisible() on the empty #elements container with toBeAttached() (zero-height when empty is expected, not a bug)

tests.title: Delete Single Element from Middle Position
Delete All Then Verify Clean State
tests.note: Healed: replaced toBeVisible() on the empty #elements container with toBeAttached() (zero-height when empty is expected, not a bug)

tests.title: Double-Click Delete Button
tests.note: Healed: expected count corrected to 3 (double-click removes 2 adjacent buttons on this site, confirmed real behavior, see BUG-002)

tests.title: Click Delete Button Container Instead of Button
tests.note: Healed: expected count corrected -- the click position lands on the first button, not empty container space, so a button IS removed (confirmed real behavior, see BUG-003)

tests.title: Verify No Maximum Limit on Add Operations
Browser Back Button Does Not Affect State
Page Refresh Resets State
tests.note: Healed: replaced toBeVisible() on the empty #elements container with toBeAttached() (zero-height when empty is expected, not a bug)

tests.title: Keyboard Accessibility for Add Element Button
tests.note: Healed: BUG-004 was a false finding, not a real site defect -- Add Element genuinely is a <button> (confirmed via accessibility snapshot); the first Tab stop is actually the page's "Fork me on GitHub" ribbon link, so the test needed two Tab presses, not one
